fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and high analyst expectations are tempered by current unprofitability and a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY indicates strong market demand.
Analyst growth estimates are exceptionally high for the current quarter (109.4%) and fiscal year (441.7%).
PEG ratio of 0.78 suggests growth may outpace valuation.
Current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
Total cash of $439.70M exceeds total debt of $280.42M.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% show the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% highlight poor profitability metrics.
Free cash flow is negative at $-445.58M TTM.
Forward P/E of 38.03 is high, indicating aggressive growth expectations are priced in.
Beta of 3.76 suggests high stock volatility relative to the market.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive momentum, with the stock up 635.5% in six months, driven by substantial transceiver orders and analyst target increases. While the company's recent quarterly earnings missed estimates, the forward P/E of 38.03 and analyst growth estimates of 109.4% for the current quarter and 441.7% for the fiscal year suggest strong future expectations. The next earnings date is August 6, 2026, which may serve as a catalyst.
